action.reasoning: Observation: Training completes with loss dropping to ~0.28 (learning the <box>/<point> format+coords on training data), checkpoint saves+eval runs but grounding_score=0.0006 (1/1581 correct, mean_iou=0.0035, all bbox, no invalids). Workspace shows truncation but stdout confirms full 15k steps executed. Analysis: Model overfits ShowUI training distribution (low-res 256px effective, domain gap to ScreenSpot-Pro pro apps/IDE/CAD UIs); low-res processor max_pixels destroys fine UI details needed for precise grounding; val split (image-disjoint same-source) should have higher score if learning occurred - must measure local val grounding_score + sample generated_text vs targets to distinguish underfitting vs generalization failure. Low res + small data+LoRA likely insufficient for coord regression. With 3h/3 iters left, prioritize quick val diagnosis (400 samples, ~15-30min) before longer retrain. Decision: Write complete non-truncated val_eval.py (reuse predict parser+rescale+clip, add IoU/L2 metric per eval contract, sample outputs, use merged ckpt, higher max_pixels=512*28*28 for test-time res), run it to get in-domain score+examples, then decide on final train/predict updates or submit.
